difficulty.reason: Perennial, self-supporting canes, produces fruit first year with minimal care

growth_stages.name: Dormant
growth_stages.look_for: Bare brown canes sticking up from the soil

growth_stages.name: New Cane Growth
growth_stages.look_for: Green shoots emerging from the base, leaves unfurling on canes

prompt.en: How tall are the new green canes this week? Are they taller than your hand?
prompt.da: Hvor hoeje er de nye groenne stammer denne uge? Er de hoejere end din haand?

growth_stages.name: Flowering
growth_stages.look_for: Small white-pink flowers in clusters at the top of canes

prompt.en: Each little flower becomes a raspberry! How many can you count?
prompt.da: Hver lille blomst bliver til et hindbaer! Hvor mange kan du taelle?

growth_stages.name: Fruiting
growth_stages.look_for: Green berries turning pink, then deep red when ripe

prompt.en: Tug gently -- if it slides off the white cone easily, it's ripe! Eat it right there!
prompt.da: Traek forsigtigt -- hvis det glider let af den hvide kegle, er det modent! Spis det med det samme!

human_readable.en: Water well in dry spells, especially when fruiting. Prefers well-drained soil.
human_readable.da: Vand godt i toerke, isaer under frugtdannelse. Foretraekker veldraenet jord.

harvest.notes: Primocane (autumn-fruiting): fruits on first-year canes from August through October. Cut all canes to ground in late autumn.

companion_plants.notes: Chives repel Japanese beetles and aphids. Keep away from strawberries (verticillium wilt).

message.en: Raspberries need water -- fruiting will suffer if soil stays dry!
message.da: Hindbaer har brug for vand -- frugtdannelsen lider, hvis jorden forbliver toer!
